strings.meta.title: {{PROGRAM_NAME}} — Play on Your Terms
strings.meta.description: Know the odds, set your limits, and play informed. {{ORGANIZATION}}'s guide to playing on your terms.
strings.helpline.strip.label: Free, confidential support 24/7
strings.hero.sub_default: Test what you actually know about the games you play.
strings.hero.fallback.sub: Every game has math. Every player has choices. Know the odds, use the tools, and decide what works for you.
strings.odds.label: Odds & Transparency
strings.odds.title: Pick your games. See the real math.
strings.odds.blurb: Every casino game has a built-in mathematical edge. Tap the games you play to see how they compare.
strings.odds.empty: Select a game above to see the odds.
strings.myths.label: Myths vs. Math
strings.myths.title: Think you know the game?
strings.myths.blurb: Every player has heard a theory. Hot streaks, lucky seats, timing the spin. Tap your answer to see what the math actually says.
strings.tools.label: Your Tools
strings.tools.title: Features that work for you.
strings.tools.blurb: These aren't restrictions. They're controls — like adjusting settings before you play.
strings.tools.budget_lock.name: Budget lock
strings.tools.budget_lock.hook: Set your entertainment spend before you start.
strings.tools.budget_lock.detail_html:
Decide what you're comfortable spending — daily, weekly, or monthly. Lock it in and it takes effect immediately. Want to lower it? Instant. Want to raise it? There's a cooling-off period so you make that call with a clear head.
Set up →
strings.tools.session_timer.name: Session timer
strings.tools.session_timer.hook: Get a nudge when your chosen time is up.
strings.tools.session_timer.detail_html:
Pick a session length that works for you. When time's up, you get a check-in — not a shutdown, just a moment to decide if you want to keep going. Stay aware without watching the clock.
Set up →
strings.tools.play_dashboard.name: Play dashboard
strings.tools.play_dashboard.hook: Full visibility into your play. No surprises.
strings.tools.play_dashboard.detail_html:
See your deposits, withdrawals, session time, and net position in one place. Full transparency over your account. Information you can act on — or just know.
View dashboard →
strings.tools.safety_net.name: Safety net
strings.tools.safety_net.hook: Set a floor. If you hit it, play pauses.
strings.tools.safety_net.detail_html:
Loss limits let you define a maximum you're willing to lose in a session, day, or week. If you hit it, play pauses automatically. You set the number — you're in control.
Set up →
strings.tools.reality_check.name: Reality check
strings.tools.reality_check.hook: Quick updates on time and spend while you play.
strings.tools.reality_check.detail_html:
Periodic pop-ups showing how long you've been playing and your current net position. Quick, non-intrusive, and easy to dismiss. Information, not interruption.
Set up →
strings.tools.budget_planner.name: Budget planner
strings.tools.budget_planner.hook: Do the math first, play after.
strings.tools.budget_planner.detail_html:
Figure out what entertainment spend fits your finances. Think about it like a night out or a streaming subscription — decide the number before you start, not after.
Plan your budget →
strings.break.label: Your Call
strings.break.title: Need some time away? It's one click.
strings.break.blurb: If you want a break — for any reason, for any length — you can step away on your terms. No forms. No questions asked.
strings.break.card_timeframe.title: Choose your timeframe
strings.break.card_timeframe.desc: Options range from a few months to permanent. Pick what feels right. You can always revisit later.
strings.break.card_pause.title: Everything pauses
strings.break.card_pause.desc: Your account pauses. Marketing stops. No deposits, no bets. A clean break from play while you want it.
strings.break.card_support.title: You're not alone
strings.break.card_support.desc: Support stays available throughout. Helplines, chat, counseling — none of that goes away when you take a break.
strings.support.label: Support
strings.support.title: Here if you need it.
strings.support.blurb: Free, confidential, 24/7. No reason needed. No crisis required.
strings.support.normalizer: Thousands of people reach out every day. It's a normal thing to do.
strings.support.org.desc: Professional support and resources for players and families.
strings.support.ga.title: Gamblers Anonymous
strings.support.ga.desc: Peer support meetings — online and in person.
strings.support.gamtalk.title: GamTalk
strings.support.gamtalk.desc: Online peer support forum — anonymous, judgement-free.
strings.hero.cta_keep_going: Keep going — take the full quiz →
strings.myths.perfect_score: Perfect score. You know your stuff. But even knowing the math doesn't change the odds — it just means you're playing with your eyes open.
strings.myths.good_effort: Whether you got them all right or not, now you know the math. And knowing the math is always better than guessing.
strings.odds.insight_pattern_html: The difference? Over 100 bets at $10 each, you'd expect to lose about ${best_loss} on {best_game} vs. ${worst_loss} on {worst_game}. Same budget, very different math.

glossary.house edge: The mathematical advantage the casino has over the player, expressed as a percentage. A 5% house edge means the casino expects to keep $5 of every $100 wagered over time.
glossary.rtp: Return to Player — the percentage of wagered money a game returns to players over time. An RTP of 96% means the game returns $96 for every $100 wagered on average.
glossary.rng: Random Number Generator — software that ensures every spin, hand, or draw is completely independent and random.
glossary.bankroll: The amount of money a player has set aside for gambling — like an entertainment budget.
glossary.volatility: How much a game's outcomes swing in the short term. High volatility = bigger wins and losses, less frequent payouts.
glossary.expected value: The mathematical average outcome of a bet if placed infinite times. A negative EV means the house has an edge.
glossary.paytable: A chart showing the payouts for each winning combination on a slot or video poker game.
glossary.wagering requirement: The number of times a bonus must be wagered before it can be withdrawn as cash.
glossary.deposit limit: A maximum amount you can deposit into your account over a set period. You set the number.
glossary.parlay: A single bet combining multiple selections. All selections must win for the bet to pay out.
glossary.tilt: A state of emotional frustration that leads to poor decision-making — when emotion takes over from math.
glossary.cool-off period: A short-term voluntary break from gambling, typically 24 hours to 30 days.
glossary.ggr: Gross Gaming Revenue — the total amount wagered minus the total amount paid out to players. The operator's top-line income.
glossary.ev: Expected Value — the average amount a player can expect to win or lose per bet over many plays.
glossary.self-exclusion: A voluntary program that lets players ban themselves from gambling venues or platforms for a set period.
glossary.session limit: A time limit you set on how long you play in a single session. A nudge when your time is up.
glossary.loss limit: A cap on how much you can lose within a set period. Once hit, play pauses automatically.
glossary.reality check: A periodic pop-up that reminds you how long you've been playing and how much you've spent.

labels.source: Source: Playbook · CC0 Licensed · Verify for your jurisdiction

facts: Every spin is independent. The machine has no memory.
RTP is set in software and regulated. Time of day is irrelevant.
Higher bets = bigger swings, not better odds.
Near misses are not “almost winning.” The result was already decided.
Lowest house edge of any casino game with basic strategy.
Basic strategy reduces house edge. It’s math, not intuition.
Card counting is real but yields a tiny 0.5–1.5% edge over thousands of hands.
The dealer follows mandatory rules with zero discretion.
American roulette (00): 5.26% house edge.
European roulette (single 0): 2.70% house edge.
Every spin is independent. The ball doesn’t know where it landed before.
No number is “due.” All 37/38 slots are equally likely.
The vig (juice) is the sportsbook’s margin. Standard: -110 on both sides.
Knowing the sport helps, but the line already prices in expertise.
Parlays compound the vig per leg. More legs = worse math.
Tipsters show highlights, not losses. Survivorship bias is real.
Banker bet: 1.06% house edge (best bet in the game).
Player bet: 1.24% house edge.
Tie bet: 14.4% house edge — avoid it.
Pattern tracking doesn’t change the odds. Each hand is independent.
With optimal strategy, some variants offer RTP over 99%.
Jacks or Better (9/6) is the gold standard: 99.54% RTP.
Unlike slots, your decisions actually affect the outcome.
Always play max coins — the Royal Flush bonus requires it.
Pass/Don’t Pass: 1.41% house edge (best bets).
Odds bet: 0% house edge — the only fair bet in the casino.
Proposition bets look exciting but carry house edges up to 16.7%.
Dice are random. Controlled shooting is a myth.
Lotteries have the highest house edge of any gambling product.
Buying 10 tickets multiplies near-zero odds. 10 × near-zero ≈ near-zero.
No number combination is more likely than any other. Randomness doesn’t have favorites.
The expected return is typically $0.40–$0.60 per $1 spent.
Odds depend on how many cards are in play, not which ones you hold.
More cards = slightly better odds, but you’re competing against all players.
Numbers are drawn randomly. No pattern or strategy changes the math.
The house takes its cut from the prize pool — typically 5–25%.
